requestedOutcome: Implement docs/aegis/plans/2026-06-02-frontend-backend-gap-remediation-plan.md and verify the frontend/backend gap remediation work.
scope: P0/P1 remediation for Chinese text and user-visible contracts, feature gates, endpoint manifest extraction, model configuration source clarity, knowledge operations, metadata governance service ownership, AI Infra tab-level degradation, runtime health visibility, and selected P2 direct external API retirement.
riskHints: Frontend routes, service contracts, and admin navigation must remain compatible with backend feature gates.
Backend controller and starter changes affect runtime capability visibility.
Test fixture ID type drift can hide real contract regressions if fixed at the wrong layer.
goal: Align frontend capability behavior with backend source-of-truth contracts and verify the result with frontend tests, backend test-source compilation, backend production compilation, and targeted browser/render checks.
successEvidence: Backend starter production reactor test-compile passes.
Aegis checkpoint, evidence, and reflection records are updated with residual risk.
stopCondition: Implemented scope is verified or explicit residual risk is recorded for checks requiring authenticated backend/full deployment state.
nonGoals: Do not remove existing public admin routes in bulk.
Do not remove backend legacy paths or /api/** aliases in this round.
Do not introduce real secrets into docs, tests, or frontend responses.
Do not fake ES, Milvus, pgvector, or provider runtime state in the frontend.
Do not claim live external adapter network health probes.
